task_completion.description: Create required layers with specific colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows layers named WALL, DRAINAGE, DIMENSIONS, and LABELS. The colors should correspond to White, Cyan, Green, and Yellow respectively.

task_completion.description: Draw the Base Footing and Wall Stem on the WALL layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the main drawing canvas, there are white lines forming a wide horizontal rectangle (footing) and a tall vertical rectangle (stem) resting on top of the footing, offset from the left edge.

task_completion.description: Draw the Gravel Drain and Drain Pipe on the DRAINAGE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the canvas, there are cyan lines forming a tall vertical rectangle (gravel drain) immediately to the right of the wall stem, and a small circle (drain pipe) located inside the lower part of this gravel drain rectangle.

task_completion.description: Add text annotations on the LABELS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Yellow text annotations are visible on the drawing, containing words like 'CONCRETE', 'GRAVEL', 'PIPE', and 'FOOTING', placed near their respective geometric components.

task_completion.description: Add linear dimensions on the DIMENSIONS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Green dimension lines and numbers are visible, indicating sizes such as 1500, 300, and 1800. At least 3 distinct dimensions should be present.
The LibreCAD window title bar or a file manager window shows the file saved with the name 'retaining_wall_section.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with LibreCAD's tools, menus, and drawing canvas, rather than just typing commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass manual drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python script (e.g., using ezdxf) or other automated tools in the terminal to generate the DXF file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must contain a wall footing (1500x300) and stem (300x1800) on the WALL layer (White). The DRAINAGE layer (Cyan) must contain a gravel drain rectangle (300x1800) to the right of the stem, and a drain pipe circle (radius ~75mm) centered near the bottom of the gravel drain. The LABELS layer (Yellow) must include text with keywords: CONCRETE, GRAVEL, PIPE, FOOTING. The DIMENSIONS layer (Green) must show at least 3 linear dimensions (e.g., 1500, 300, 1800). The file must be saved as 'retaining_wall_section.dxf'.
